items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. Successful exploitation could lead to arbitrary code execution, privilege escalation, and security feature bypass.

items.title: File Upload Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: A critical file upload vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to privilege escalation. This issue was addressed as part of APSB26-73.

items.title: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: A critical improper encoding or escaping of output vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to arbitrary code execution. This issue was addressed as part of APSB26-73.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: This critical vulnerability in the Mirasvit Cache Warmer extension allows unauthenticated PHP object injection via a crafted cookie, leading to remote code execution. It has been actively exploited in the wild.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization Vulnerability in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: An incorrect authorization vulnerability in Adobe Commerce allows unauthenticated attackers to bypass security checks and gain unauthorized write access to the application. Adobe published this vulnerability with a CVSS 3.1 base score of 7.5.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files disguised as images, leading to remote code execution. Mass exploitation attempts have been observed against vulnerable stores.

items.title: Critical Security Update: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This critical security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source addresses multiple vulnerabilities. Successful exploitation could lead to security feature bypass, denial-of-service, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read.
